name: Fiber Reinforced Polyurethane FRPU Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Fiber Reinforced Polyurethane FRPU. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for FRPU cleaning without thermal damage to fiber reinforcement

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for polyurethane absorption while minimizing fiber damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control on FRPU surface

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for efficient cleaning with minimal thermal accumulation

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective polyurethane removal while preserving fibers

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of polyurethane matrix

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for efficient coverage without excessive dwell time

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive thermal input

power.description: Typical range for polymer composites like FRPU to balance cleaning efficiency and avoid thermal degradation; starting at 100W for light contamination. Max safe ~150-200W before substrate melting (polyurethane degrades ~200°C). Source: IPG Photonics fiber laser specs for cleaning; studies on polymer ablation (e.g., J. Appl. Phys. 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective for contamination removal on low-conductivity FRPU; slower for heavy contamination (500-1000 mm/s), faster for light (2000-3000 mm/s). Recommended 1500 mm/s for optimal balance. Source: Industry standards from Laserax and Adapt-Laser manuals for composite cleaning.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning of polymers; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-minimal HAZ but overkill for FRPU. 50 ns minimizes heat-affected zone while ablating contaminants. Source: Laser physics research on polymer ablation (Opt. Lasers Eng. 2020).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed fiber lasers in cleaning; 40 kHz recommended for FRPU to enable fast processing without excessive heat accumulation in low-thermal-conductivity material. Higher rates (>60 kHz) risk buildup. Source: Academic literature on composite laser processing (Composites Part B, 2019).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant removal on FRPU; damage threshold ~1.5-2.5 J/cm² due to polyurethane's low melting point (~180-250°C) and moderate 1064 nm absorption. Recommended 0.8 J/cm² for cleaning without substrate harm. Source: Ablation threshold studies (Appl. Surf. Sci. 2017).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise cleaning on FRPU surfaces; smaller (50-100 μm) for fine details, larger (200-300 μm) for broad areas. 150 μm recommended for uniform coverage in industrial prep. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-4) for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete FRPU cleaning to ensure removal without overexposure. Source: Industry case studies on polymer composite cleaning (J. Clean. Prod. 2021).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage on irregular FRPU surfaces; 60% recommended for even cleaning without gaps or excessive overlap-induced heat. Typical 30-80% range. Source: Process optimization studies (Opt. Laser Technol. 2019).
